Your Request for Advice
Our FiIe No. W-05-027
Dear Ms. Peak
This letter is in response to your request for advice regarding the conflict of
interest provisions of the Political Reform Act (the "Act")l on behalf of the members of
the San Marcos City Council. In your letter, you ask if a rnember of the city council may
vote on his or her own appointment by the city council to a joint powers agency if the
member disclaims or waives the stipend that the position would otherwise carry. you
concluded by stating that you "have informed the city council of the intent to submit this
request for advice, and the council has proceeded to make the appointments with the
caveat that they are subject to the Commission,s response.,,
Please be advised that the Commission will not provide advice with respect to
past conduct, such as that relating to votes that have already taken place. (Regulation
18329(bX8)(A), copy enclosed.) As the vote(s) pertaining to the issue raised in yo,r.
letter has already taken place, the Commission is unable to provide advice on this matter.
t Govemment Code sections 81000 - 91014. Commission regulations appear at Title 2, sections
I 8109- 1 8997, of the Califomia Code of Regulations. All statutory references herein are to the Government
Code unless otherwise indicated. All regulatory references herein are to Title 2 of the California Code of
Regulations unless otherwise indicated.


For your information, we have enclosed thepamphlet ,.can r yoter' a guide to
preventing conflicts of interest for public officials. If you have any other q,r".iio6
regarding this matter, please contact me at (916) 322-5660.
Sincerely

sections.question: May a member of the city council vote on his or her own appointment by the city council to a joint powers agency if the member disclaims or waives the stipend that the position would otherwise carry?
sections.conclusion: The Commission will not provide advice with respect to past conduct, such as that relating to votes that have already taken place. As the vote(s) pertaining to the issue raised in your letter has already taken place, the Commission is unable to provide advice on this matter.
sections.question_synthetic: The City Attorney of San Marcos requests advice on whether city council members can vote on their own appointments to a joint powers agency if they waive the associated stipend, citing the Political Reform Act's conflict of interest provisions.
sections.conclusion_synthetic: The FPPC declined to provide advice because the votes in question had already occurred, as FPPC regulations prohibit advice regarding past conduct.
sections.parsing_notes: No section headers found; LLM: Document is properly formatted advice letter with clear Question and Conclusion sections. File number is W-05-027 (W prefix indicates withdrawal or declination category). FPPC declined jurisdiction based on regulatory prohibition against advising on past conduct.

embedding.qa_text:
QUESTION: May a member of the city council vote on his or her own appointment by the city council to a joint powers agency if the member disclaims or waives the stipend that the position would otherwise carry?

CONCLUSION: The Commission will not provide advice with respect to past conduct, such as that relating to votes that have already taken place. As the vote(s) pertaining to the issue raised in your letter has already taken place, the Commission is unable to provide advice on this matter.

embedding.summary: The FPPC declined to advise on whether city council members can vote on their own joint powers agency appointments while waiving stipends because the votes had already taken place and the FPPC does not provide advice on past conduct.
